description: VAL-ALIGN-004: Global advantage norm, VAL-ALIGN-005: Token-level KL, VAL-ALIGN-006: Trust region clipping, VAL-ALIGN-007: Loss differentiable, VAL-ALIGN-008: Memory budget

tests: test_normalizes_to_zero_mean: Normalized advantages have zero mean
test_normalizes_to_unit_std: Normalized advantages have unit std
VAL-ALIGN-004.observations: global_normalize_advantages() normalizes across the entire batch using global mean and std. Test 'test_global_not_per_group' explicitly proves normalization is not per-group by showing group means are non-zero after global normalization.

tests: test_shape_is_batch_seq_len: KL tensor shape is (batch, seq_len)
test_varying_batch_size: Shape invariant under batch size
VAL-ALIGN-005.observations: token_level_kl() computes D_KL(policy || reference) = exp(log_pi) * (log_pi - log_ref), clamped to min=0 for numerical stability. Output shape is (batch, seq_len), all values non-negative.

tests: test_extreme_ratios_clipped: Extreme log-ratios (exp(10)) clipped to bounds
test_moderate_ratios_unchanged: Ratios already in bounds preserved
VAL-ALIGN-006.observations: clip_log_ratio() computes ratio = exp(log_pi - log_ref) and clamps to [clip_low, clip_high]. Default bounds are [0.8, 1.2] (clip_range=0.2). Extreme ratios (e.g., exp(10) ≈ 22026) are clipped correctly.

tests: test_zero_kl_when_identical: KL term vanishes when policy==reference
test_higher_kl_increases_loss: Higher kl_coeff increases total loss
VAL-ALIGN-007.observations: reinforce_pp_loss() returns a scalar with requires_grad=True. Supports backward() and gradients propagate through log-prob computation to input logits. Combines clipped policy gradient + KL penalty.

tests: test_memory_budget_cpu_proxy: Pipeline (policy+reference, no critic) with batch=1, seq_len=128 uses < 12GB RAM
test_pipeline_has_no_critic: No critic_model or value_model attribute
VAL-ALIGN-008.observations: CPU proxy test with a tiny model (vocab=320, d_model=64) verifies pipeline structure: creates policy + reference (no critic). tracemalloc confirms peak memory well under 12GB. Uses TinyModel as proxy since actual 1B model is too slow for CPU unit tests.
